title: centaurea paniculata subsp. bertolonii
intro: Centaurea paniculata subsp. bertolonii is a subspecies of the Centaurea paniculata plant, commonly known as the Mountain centaury, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea paniculata subsp. bertolonii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antimicrobial agent. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its properties and potential applications.
